name: From Assafora to Praia da Samarra — circular route in Parque Natural Sintra-Cascais
name_original: De Assafora à Praia da Samarra — percurso circular no Parque Natural Sintra-Cascais
region_intro: Hiking around São João Das Lampas offers diverse landscapes within and around the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park. The region features a mosaic of agricultural areas, pine forests, and rural roads, some with Roman origins. Hikers can experience both coastal paths with Atlantic Ocean views and inland trails that pass traditional stone walls, streams, and windmills. The terrain includes areas with waterfalls and varying elevations, providing a range of experiences for outdoor enthusiasts. Best hiking trails around São João Das Lampas The most popular hiking route is From Assafora to Praia da Samarra — circular route in Parque Natural Sintra-Cascais, a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 30 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers varied scenery from agricultural fields to coastal views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Rota das Aldeias — circular route PR 9 SNT in Parque Natural Sintra-Cascais, a moderate 8.0 miles (13.0 km) path. This trail showcases the region's traditional "saloia" countryside and agricultural landscape. Local hikers also love the Magoito Beach – Praia da Samarra loop from Duna Consolidada da Praia do Magoito, a 7.6 miles (12.2 km) trail leading through coastal dunes and offering sea views, often completed in about 3 hours 33 minutes. Hiking around São João Das Lampas is defined by coastal paths, pine forests, and agricultural areas within the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging moderate trails. The routes in São João Das Lampas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 1,400 hikers have used komoot to explore São João Das Lampas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in São João Das Lampas?
A: There are over 50 hiking trails around São João Das Lampas, offering a diverse range of experiences within and near the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from easy walks to more challenging moderate trails.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, São João Das Lampas offers many easy trails, with 24 routes specifically categorized as easy. The region is known for its accessible paths, making it suitable for families and those new to hiking. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379922323/rota-das-aldeias-circular-route-pr-9-snt-in-parque-natural-sintra-cascais" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rota das Aldeias — circular route PR 9 SNT&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail that showcases the traditional countryside and is generally considered suitable for all skill levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on the trails around São João Das Lampas?
A: The trails in São João Das Lampas offer a rich mosaic of landscapes. You'll encounter agricultural areas with farmsteads, dense pine forests, and rural roads, some with Roman origins. Hikers can experience both dramatic coastal paths with Atlantic Ocean views and inland routes featuring traditional stone walls, serene streams, fountains, and historic windmills.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, São João Das Lampas is well-known for its circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379922323/rota-das-aldeias-circular-route-pr-9-snt-in-parque-natural-sintra-cascais" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rota das Aldeias — circular route PR 9 SNT&lt;/a&gt;, which starts and ends in São João Das Lampas itself. Another excellent choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380012075/from-assafora-to-praia-da-samarra-circular-route-in-parque-natural-sintra-cascais" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Assafora to Praia da Samarra — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, offering varied scenery from agricultural fields to coastal views.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: Many trails in the São João Das Lampas region are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas like the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park, and to check specific trail regulations before you go.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls or coastal views?
A: Absolutely. The area around São João Das Lampas is known for its scenic coastal paths and routes featuring waterfalls. You can find trails that offer stunning views of the Atlantic Ocean, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1269535" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magoito Beach – Praia da Samarra loop&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through coastal dunes. Some routes also feature eye-catching cascades and serene streams, like those found near the Roman bridge at Catribana.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions near the hiking routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, the region boasts several historical and cultural landmarks. You can explore significant sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/205133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Moors&lt;/a&gt;, the enchanting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/185113" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quinta da Regaleira&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/205132" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;National Palace of Sintra&lt;/a&gt;. These attractions offer a glimpse into the rich heritage of the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park area.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in São João Das Lampas?
A: São João Das Lampas offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for exploring. Summer can be warm, especially inland, but coastal trails often benefit from refreshing sea breezes. Winter hikes are also possible, with the landscape taking on a different, often greener, charm.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the trailheads, or is parking usually accessible?
A: While some trailheads might be accessible by local public transport, having a car often provides more flexibility for reaching various starting points around São João Das Lampas. Many popular trailheads, especially those within the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park, typically have accessible parking areas. It's advisable to check specific route details for parking information.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in São João Das Lampas?
A: The hiking trails in São João Das Lampas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 250 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse scenery, which includes coastal views, pine forests, and traditional agricultural landscapes. The variety of routes catering to different abilities and the well-maintained paths are also commonly highlighted.

Q: Are there any amenities like cafes or restaurants near the hiking trails?
A: São João Das Lampas and its surrounding villages offer various amenities. You'll find local cafes and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your hike, often serving traditional Portuguese cuisine. These establishments are typically located within the villages or along main roads, providing convenient stops for hikers.
tips: Small lagoon in the village of Assafora. There is a small square next to the lake where some ducks and geese approach if they suspect there is bread nearby. Very peaceful place.
Ponta dos Ladouros is a difficult to access place close to Praia da Samarra. From this point, however, it is possible to admire the place from the top of the cliff.
Praia da Samarra is one of the most "hidden" beaches in the region because access is not the simplest. Famous for its elongated shape between two cliffs. A show.
